---
title: "Annotate cell types"
description: "Assign biological cell-type labels to clusters or cells."
categories: [openpipeline]
order: 45
---

[`openpipeline`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline}<span class="swatch openpipeline"></span>

After [integration](integrate.qmd) the clusters are just numbers. Annotation
assigns biological cell-type labels, either from known markers by hand, or
automatically by transferring labels from an annotated reference.

## Options

| Workflow / component | Use when |
|---|---|
| [`annotation/celltypist`](../reference/openpipeline/v4.2.0/workflows/annotation/celltypist.qmd) | Pretrained models (esp. immune); no reference of your own |
| [`annotation/harmony_knn`](../reference/openpipeline/v4.2.0/workflows/annotation/harmony_knn.qmd) | KNN label transfer from a reference (Harmony space) |
| [`annotation/scvi_knn`](../reference/openpipeline/v4.2.0/workflows/annotation/scvi_knn.qmd) | KNN label transfer in scVI latent space |
| [`annotation/scanvi_scarches`](../reference/openpipeline/v4.2.0/workflows/annotation/scanvi_scarches.qmd) | scANVI / scArches; preserves query-specific biology |
| [`annotate/singler`](../reference/openpipeline/v4.2.0/annotate/singler.qmd) | Correlation-based transfer from a Bioconductor reference |
| [`single_cell/parallel_annotation`](../reference/openpipeline_composed/v0.3.0/single_cell/parallel_annotation.qmd) | Run several methods and take a consensus |

## Example: CellTypist

For example, CellTypist ships pretrained models (browse them at
[celltypist.org](https://www.celltypist.org/models)), so it needs no reference of
your own:

```bash
nextflow run https://packages.viash-hub.com/vsh/openpipeline.git \
  -r 4.2.0 -latest -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/workflows/annotation/celltypist/main.nf \
  --id cohort_01 \
  --input cohort_01_integrated.h5mu \
  --model "Immune_All_Low.pkl" \
  --output cohort_01_annotated.h5mu \
  --publish_dir results/
```

## Key choices

- **Marker-based vs. reference-based.** Marker-based gives full control but needs
  expertise and doesn't scale; reference-based is automated and reproducible.
- **Which reference.** Use a pretrained CellTypist model, a public atlas, or your
  own annotated dataset. A reference atlas can itself be built with the turnkey
  pipeline, see [Run a turnkey pipeline](turnkey.qmd#generate-a-reference-atlas).
- **Consensus.** `parallel_annotation` runs several methods and votes, robust when
  no single method is clearly best.

## Output

Predicted labels land in `.obs` (e.g. `<target>_pred`) alongside a probability
column. Downstream analyses that need cell types, [differential
expression](differential-expression.qmd) and
[cell-cell communication](cell-cell-communication.qmd), read from here.
